steps: resolve the rate-limited endpoint, the documented key header and key value, the documented limit N, and the documented window from the runtime brief by role
issue exactly N read-only GETs carrying the documented key inside a single window via the harness
assert every one of the N burst requests returns the documented success class
record each response and the RateLimit-* header values for the header-decrement baseline
resolve the rate-limited endpoint and the documented key header and value by role
issue one more read-only GET (request number N+1) with the same key immediately after the burst via the harness
assert the request is throttled with the documented throttle status class
record the throttled response for the report
resolve the rate-limited endpoint and the documented window and key by role
wait until two seconds before the window is expected to close
issue one read-only GET with the exhausted key via the harness
assert the request is still limited because the window has not yet reopened
resolve the rate-limited endpoint and the documented window and key by role
wait until one second after the window is expected to open
issue one read-only GET with the same key via the harness
assert the request succeeds because the allowance has reset for the new window
resolve the rate-limited endpoint, the documented key header, and a second independent key value by role
with the first key already exhausted, issue N read-only GETs carrying the second key inside one window via the harness
assert every request under the second key returns the documented success class
assert the second key's allowance is unaffected by the first key's exhaustion (per-key isolation)
resolve the rate-limited endpoint, a documented sibling endpoint, and the documented limit scope (per-endpoint vs global) by role
exhaust the allowance on the primary endpoint, then issue a read-only GET against the sibling endpoint with the same key via the harness
if the documented scope is global, assert the sibling request is throttled because it shares the counter
if the documented scope is per-endpoint, assert the sibling request succeeds because it keeps a separate counter
resolve the rate-limited endpoint, the documented key, and the documented RateLimit-Limit/RateLimit-Remaining/RateLimit-Reset (or X-RateLimit-*) header names by role
walk the N burst responses recorded from the at-limit case via the harness
assert the documented RateLimit-* headers are present on every burst response
assert RateLimit-Remaining decrements by exactly one per request across the burst, RateLimit-Limit equals the documented limit N, and RateLimit-Reset is present
